text: My car has been in the shop because I drive an Audi and I’ve been using Zipcar for the last week and it’s been awesome.… Last Saturday I needed to take a zip car for three appointments and it worked out brilliantly. And the zip is so close to my house. It’s very simple to pick one up and drop it off again and then go right home. They are always clean. Gas is free and to me. It’s a very fair trade for using the car for a few hours and not overnight. I recommend the new Camry hybrid to anyone. It’s surprisingly awesome and handles better than a German car.

The non-refundable fee is just a scam, and asked me for more information that at this point is nonsense. Better to rent a car somewhere else.

title: Potentially racially motivated account deletion
text: I don’t usually write reviews but I made an account because I had an important meeting to catch that was far from me and an uber would’ve been too expensive. My colleagues recommended Zipcar to me so I downloaded it, set it up scanned my DL and took the selfie. Day before my meeting my account gets “marked for review” for no reason I haven’t had the account for a full day yet AND then the day of it was “temporarily suspended.” I carried customer service and the last I talked with explained it was a “security procedure” to flag and suspend user accounts for no reason with no warning making them unable to use their app and that just makes no sense to me because none of these reviews mention it. Then this morning I get an email saying my account was deleted (again mind you, I never got a chance to add my payment information, let alone book a car). Needless to say I’m deleting the app and telling everyone I know to use hertz or uber rentals instead. The only reason I’m marking the as racially motivated is because it makes NO sense why my account was marked for review, suspended, or deleted. And if course in the email the security team can’t say why for “security reasons” (B.S).

text: I rented a Hyundai Ioniq Electric car/Zipcar through the Uber program. It took them 9 hours into the 24 hour rental period to put the car onto my Uber account. Once I was finally able to do that, I start driving and then the car starts to jerk and won’t accelerate correctly, and warning lights come on saying check electric vehicle system. title: Nothing but hassle.
text: I reinstated my membership after seeing a car back in my neighborhood, only to find the vehicle had a flat rear passenger tire during my exterior inspection at 10:00 AM. I understand flats happen, but this left me unable to make the trip I had planned and effectively wasted a monthly membership payment I had just been charged. When I asked about a refund, support said it would take 3–5 business days — fine — but declined any promotional compensation for the inconvenience. This isn’t the first time a Zipcar has broken down on me; on a previous occasion I had to take an Uber home. Between the recurring reliability issues and the lack of meaningful customer accommodation, this ghetto experience has convinced me I won’t be using Zipcar again.

title: Disappointed Beyond All Measure
text: I’m currently without a car and had been using Zipcar to make a weekly run over several weeks. One week I tried to make a reservation for my weekly run and the card I was using was not being accepted. It turned out that I needed to update something with my bank and I did so. I attempted to make the reservation again and it still didn’t go through. I transferred money to another payment method and made the reservation. I went to double check the reservation and the reservation was canceled. I called ZipCar to find out what was going on and was told my account was suspended pending an investigation and to call back on Monday, April 6,2026. I called back and was told that the account was closed and I was banned from using ZipCar permanently. I asked why and was never given an explanation. I asked to speak to a supervisor and was told there wasn’t any supervisor. I was told if I wanted to appeal the decision I could respond to an email that was to be sent out. I got the email and responded to it only to get an error message saying that the email could not be responded to. I attempted to contact Zipcar through their Facebook account and got the run around. I don’t know what happened and I at least deserved an explanation and didn’t get that much. I’m paying double using Uber and Lyft to make that same run and I just want to say thank you for nothing and I will forever tell people not to bother with Zipcar.

title: On of the Greatest Smart Mobility Solutions
text: Zipcar has consistently been one of the most practical and innovative mobility solutions available. The ability to reserve a vehicle by the hour or day through a simple app makes transportation flexible, affordable, and convenient without the costs and responsibilities of car ownership.  The vehicles are generally clean, well-maintained, and located in convenient areas throughout the city. For professionals, students, travelers, and occasional drivers, Zipcar provides an excellent balance of accessibility, reliability, and value. The reservation process is straightforward, pickup and return are seamless, and the variety of vehicle options helps meet different transportation needs.  As urban transportation continues to evolve, Zipcar remains a leading example of smart, sustainable, and user-friendly mobility. Highly recommended for anyone seeking convenient on-demand transportation.

title: 80% Chance of having issues
text: The app as well as the Zipcar service are easily a time suck. In many cases the app doesn’t actually open the car door which adds another 15 minutes to getting on your way. Other issues in the app error out with  ‘something went wrong’. A more specific error message might help avoiding an equally painful interaction with the Zipcar call center. All in all every interaction with Zipcar has a high chance of frustration - I guess that’s what happens if a company becomes  a de facto monopoly.

title: Good concept, Poor execution
text: The pros of this app are that it really helps to grab a car when you need it. The downs of the app are it glitches. The app is clunky. Worst of all is the experience when you need to connect to support via the app. When you connect to support it, takes you within the app to a browser page. Then you engage with an AI bot, which is a terrible experience. The AI bot cannot answer any questions, the categories are confusing and worst of all when you need to talk to an agent, which always happens because of how the bot is set up, you then have to sign into Zipcar, even though you're signed in already via the app. This is a huge source of frustration, is completely unhelpful, drags the process out, makes it complicated, and makes no sense at all. This is an easy fix, yet the Zipcar team is not investing in this.

We tried speaking to a supervisor but they wouldn’t let us speak to them and continuously put us on hold. I’ve already had issues with zipcar in the past and customer service was rude to me. title: Use enterprise or a car legit rental service
text: Cars are DISGUSTING weed and tobacco smoke. Burn marks on leather, dirt everywhere, trash in car. Dirty CLOTHES in car. Mileage is past due for oil change (the app literally tracks the car, they know the mileage.) Dings and scratches all over car, clearly needs maintenance. Car had no gas in it. Costs MORE than a car from enterprise for the same amount of time. Parking spot was taken by some random person so when I called customer service they told me to find another spot not in the parking garage that was 24/7, within a mile, and free. I’m in a huge city, this isn’t a thing. I later realize that due to their lack of understanding of the English language they meant ‘legal’ when they said ‘free’. No reimbursement only credit, as if I’d want to use this service again. Finally after explaining to the foreign call center person how parking garages work they said I could park it in another spot. I’m realizing this service is for people that can’t qualify for real rental services. If you can qualify for a real rental service, then use that. This is what I get for trying to do something new. Back to enterprise I go.

title: 50/50 on the experience
text: I feel in love with Zipcar when I was traveling for school in nyc and it was such an easy means of travel. With that being said the only thing I hated about the app is how unreliable it is. I can’t to the mind of me understand how and when I book a car and I go to that pick-up location and the car is not there. Customer service albeit respectful for the most part don’t seem to know where their own car is as well, which shows a lack of communication within their team. It’s really my only issue with them. At least give me a notification that “hey your booked car is not at the location” rather than having me go there and there’s no car, so now I wasted my time looking for their property. I would recommend booking cars that are in a parking lot. But overall it’s a decent app if you’re trying to go somewhere quickly.

title: Buyer Beware!
text: My wife and I were headed upstate to an Airbnb and rented a Zipcar to get up there as we do now own a car. As Brooklyn residents we’ve used their service for years. They’ve recently shifted from providing you with a physical key to having a key fob locked and hidden somewhere within the vehicle. When attempting to start the car again on our journey the key fob battery was dead and the car would no longer start. I figured that’s a $5 fix for a new battery.   I contacted customer support and was told they could not disclose where the key is located in the car for “security purposes” and would have to arrange for the vehicle to be towed. However rather than being able to use the tow company a mile down the road, they could only use their authorized tow company which would take a few days to pick up the vehicle. They essentially left my wife and I stranded. What could had been a $5 battery replacement turned into multiple hundred of dollars to tow the vehicle. I do not understand why Zipcar will gladly abandon their vehicles and their customers for days on end.

title: Do not use Zipcar, use Turo

When I returned that car, there was a car parked in the Zipcar spot! I parked nearby, called Zipcar, told them where I parked it (a short term spot), and then a month later got emailed a parking ticket for that spot! I have rented with Turo many times and never had these issues. Do not use Zipcar if at all possible.

title: They really don’t value customer loyalty
text: I’ve been a customer of this service for a few years now. At first, things were okay, but over time, I ran into a lot of issues. They took unauthorized funds from my account more than once due to mileage miscalculations and took days to fix it without offering any meaningful compensation.  When I moved to Dallas, it got even more frustrating. I’d arrive at a location only to find out the car wasn’t available. The whole point of this service is supposed to be renting a car on demand, and that’s the last thing I’ve experienced. Instead of getting a quick, convenient rental, I’ve had to deal with constant miscommunications and last-minute changes.  On top of that, there’s a real lack of membership loyalty. They’re quick to collect monthly fees, but when it comes to offering real solutions or compensation, they fall short. Most of the time, I had to be the one to suggest canceling because they wouldn’t offer any real resolution.  In short, this service could be so much better if they actually focused on the core promise of on-demand rentals and valued their loyal customers. Right now, it just doesn’t feel like they do.

title: Unreliable
text: I’ve been using Zipcar on and off for about two years, and unfortunately my experiences have been unreliable. On multiple occasions, Zipcar has taken money that didn’t belong to them and, while they did eventually refund it, the process always came with poor customer service.  The first issue happened when I booked a 7-hour trip (11 p.m. to 7 a.m.), but their system changed my reservation from a 6-hour booking down to only 1 hour. I was still charged the 6-hour price for that shortened trip, and then they tried to charge me again for the original 7-hour reservation. They actually took the money from my account before eventually refunding it.  The second issue came when they incorrectly charged me $128, claiming I didn’t return the car to the right location. In reality, I returned it on time and to the correct spot. They did refund the money, but not without putting a hold on my account. After the hold was lifted, they then deleted my account, saying I hadn’t paid the membership fee — even though I couldn’t pay it because of the hold.  Overall, while Zipcar did eventually return my money, the repeated billing errors, account issues, and poor customer service make the service unreliable.

title: Poor Communication and Unfair Treatment
text: I want to leave a clear record of my experience with Zipcar. After canceling my membership years ago (and even receiving a refund at the time), Zipcar continued to charge me annual fees without providing any service. When I contacted support, the communication was slow, inconsistent, and dismissive.  At first, Zipcar admitted the mistake and refunded one charge, but then refused to refund earlier charges even though I provided proof of my cancellation and refund history. The company’s explanations contradicted themselves — they refunded my membership in one year while claiming my account was still “active” in another. This contradiction was never explained, and my concerns were brushed off as “policy.”  In the end, they marked my case as “resolved” without ever fairly addressing the real issue. This kind of communication and refusal to take responsibility is unacceptable for a company holding people’s payment information.  If you’re thinking about using Zipcar, be very careful — keep written records, check your statements, and do not assume “cancellation” means what it should. My experience shows how poor communication and a lack of accountability can lead to years of unfair charges.

title: Dirty Cars, Dirty Tactics
text: I am a college student and having ZipCar(s) near my campus seemed like a great thing for me to use, pretty affordable and easy access in the case that I needed to drive anywhere. Unfortunately I have come to figure out that the cars are 1. Not well maintained, and 2. Not clean. I rented a simple Kia Forte for an hour and a half to run an errand, I was met with a car with visual damage that seemed a little off putting. The damage itself wasn’t enough for me to reject the car, as it was mostly cosmetic (cracks in front and rear bumpers, tennis ball sized dents on multiple locations) and so I continued with the inspection process. When I opened the car though, the smell was horrible. I thought ZipCars were supposed to be non smoking vehicles? I was met with the distinct and very recognizable smell of a car that has had a smoke smell MARINATE. It’s not a recent event in that car’s life. The car itself was also dirty on the inside, crumbs lining the floor, coins scattered in the central storage, etc. I made SURE to note all these defects in my pre-trip inspection. Unfortunately for me I couldn’t cancel and rebook as this was the only car available at the time and my errand was urgent and couldn’t wait. I had to drive with the windows down in 60 degree weather to make the smell manageable. At the end of my rental I attempt to contact the text support but am frankly left unsatisfied, so I revisit it in the morning. Support not only proceeds to have extensive waiting times for responses but also just ghosts me for around 6 hours until I’m fed up enough to call in person. While seething in the fact that support left me unanswered for so long I check my bank account, now tell me why, as a broke college student I see not one, not two, but THREE separate charges for the same amount on my card? (Thankfully one of them seemed to have been removed) Now I sure as heck do not have movement like that and can overlook an extra 30-40 bucks missing from my account. So this is when I call support. The lady in which I speak with is dejected and frankly unhelpful. When I mentioned my previous chats with text support I audibly heard her SIGH as if I’m wasting her time and day trying to solve the issue?  I was nothing but kind and respectful as I know tech support is an exhausting job to deal with but that doesn’t mean I’m going to take it kindly when you seem unhappy to DO YOUR JOB. Hopefully this app can fix itself and its ways, my money better be returned in my account in the “3-5 business days” as stated otherwise there will be a big issue, as well as the “30 minute credit” they promised as a result of the frankly nasty car. I hope others have a more positive and pleasant experience with this rental app than I have had but I think I’ll stick to walking, public transport, or other means of travel.

Tried to resend another code and nothing, eventually it locked my account for maximum resend code limit reached. Called support and they were not able to assist had to escalate the situation and the escalation takes 2-3 working days. Uber is my livelihood and the fact that I’m not able to possibly work for 2-3 days is extremely frustrating. The customer service is really bad, it should not take 2-3 business days to assist with getting people into a car. This is the only rental car service that has a wait time of that long. Every other uber rental service platform I have used, fixes issues like these immediately. Please do better, this is not an issue that is new, people have been complaining over a year that the app doesn’t sent a resend code. You are financially impacting working people in the most negative way, please invest in the quality of your app and the quality of your customer service team, they should be able to do more, don’t be cheap, spend the money to improve so you retain your customers.

text: I have rented from Zipcar three times now, and each of those three times it has been BLATANTLY OBVIOUS that smoking—and I mean a lot of smoking—has taken place in the car. In the last rental, it is CLEAR  that whoever was smoking in the passenger seat dropped there weed because there are clearly marijuana bits all on the floor. I was tempted to stop and clean the entire car myself, but it’s obvious that the next person would just dirty it up again.   Also, the cars are not well maintained at all—does anyone clean them? I had to stop at Walmart to by wipes for the windows. The windows were so dirty that, at night, I was literally afraid I was going to die because I couldn’t see anything!!!!!   And let’s not begin to talk about the sound of the engine in that Camry I got.   That said, if you NEED a quick car, this service will help you but it will NOT be a comfortable ride unless you smoke weed everyday and you like dirt.

We had to scramble! And this is the WORST part - they charged us for the time extensions they had to make for their own parking problems! title: SCAM
text: I rented a car last week and went to park in the public parking spot in my neighborhood, only to find it was blocked by another Zipcar and I had nowhere to park. I spent over an hour driving around looking for parking spots (on the phone with Zipcar support the whole time) where they recommended I park in a lot 1-4 miles away and 'walk home' despite renting the car amidst a busy day and nursing an injury. They promised repeatedly they were filing this incident on my account and I would not be charged if the car was towed or received parking tickets, only to be charged $100+ to both extend the car (which the employee lied and said was free since I spent the hour looking for parking and at a huge inconvenience to em) as well as for the parking ticket. I'm grateful I knew better than to leave it parked in a tow away zone because these scammers would've charged me $1k+ for that issue. After 5 calls, 3 emails they are now saying they don't have any record of the issue on my file (despite multiple employees seeing it before) and demanding I share evidence (which I don't have because it's a phone call). I just cancelled my membership and reported the fraud to my bank and the better business bureau. Do not use this company - they will charge you exorbitantly for things ttheir employees tell you to do, while refusing to provide any credit for the inconvenience and time lost (both trying to park and 5x calls later).

title: Dead battery 15min in
text: Picked up a Zipcar this AM from a location on the same street as my home. Got it home, loaded it with things I needed for errands that I would need to run before an important time-sensitive appointment. Got back into the Zipcar and it wouldn’t start. The battery was completely dead. No way to get me another car near my home. Day ruined.

title: Terrible Booking Service
text: What’s the point of booking the car, taking my money, and the car not being there where it’s supposed to be?? Received assistance on getting another car but it delayed my trip. Also, the card that is used to fill up the gas before you return it doesn’t even work! Cool concept but clearly something’s not working.

I have my son’s and my wife and they are freezing. The car is filthy inside and out. But I was in a crunch for time and couldn’t get another car. Now asking for manager they put on an endless hold and where still out here freezing. They just hung up on me.

title: Worst car rental service in the universe!
text: Zipcar would go down as the worst company I've ever encountered with! They charged me $400 late return fee for not being able to return the car due to an urgent matter. They suspended my account as well. Do not use this scam service ever!

title: Good!

title: a scam
text: Their business model is very similar to a scam. Failure to provide important details, doesn’t make it easy to cancel, doesn’t explain rental process well. do not recommend  .

title: Terrible Experience
text: My experience was horrible… And I’m actually very surprised! I made my reservation in advance, and when I got to the location where the car was suppose to be, it was not there. Apparently the person who had it before me didn’t bring it being back. Long story short, I ended up getting a car and, at this point it was 2 1/2 hours later from my initial reservation time.  The car I did finally get was absolutely filthy!! The seats were stained and dirty and it smelled horrible! Apparently Zipcar does not clean their cars in between rides… After many calls to customer service, I was advised that I would be refunded for the car because of the condition it was in.  Their representative sent me an email advising I’d be refunded and the it was processed… I’ve got  nothing.  My case appears close and all I’ve gotten was about $20 between a credit and a refund sent to my bank card.  This was a totally upsetting experience and I just want to be refunded and done with this.

title: WARNING: Most of their vehicles have expired registration.
text: Make sure to check the glove box with registration papers. More likely than not you will have papers that are out of date. It is illegal to drive a car in US without proper registration.

title: do not use zip car
text: if you're thinking about using zip car you should definitely reconsider you would save money by using Uber or Lyft and it would be much more convenient. Zipcar charged me $240 for what should've been a $28 trip and even if they had charged me the accurate amount it is such a stressful headache to try and find the car and return it in the allotted amount of time because you can only rent it for blocks of time starting and ending on the hour and it can take an hour just to find and start the car and then they charge you whatever they want and it has no relevance to what actually happened. I contacted Zipcar about this ridiculous charge and they say they will get back to me within 24 hours but as it stands they've taken $240 out of my bank account when I returned the car ahead of time and had it for less than two hours.

title: LOTS OF HIDDEN FEES
text: Really creative service. Love the concept, somewhat easy to use, and somewhat affordable… until you finish your ride and suddenly there are 4 rows of extra charges. Would have enjoyed the service more if they were more transparent about their pricing. I paid $20/hr for a car that was supposed to be $13/hr??? Then I had to call the help line because the car wouldn’t start, wasting the first 15min of my travel. Then I tried to extend the trip only for the app to stop working and the phone lines to go silent. I arrived 10min late and was charged $50. Upon reviewing the receipt, there were an additional $8 in random fees. A ride that was supposed to be $31 ended up being $98

title: Late fee, I didn’t even move the car
text: I rented the car for 6 hours one day, I forgot my scooter inside. Contacted Zipcar they kept telling me to meet the next person but kept giving me wrong times to show up and everytime I showed up the car wasn’t there, when I was finally able to book a car for a time and get my scooter back( keep in mind all I did was unlock the car grab my scooter then lock the car and end trip) I was charged a $65.13 late fee for that day, I contacted them to get my money back since I clearly didn’t drop it off late. They told me “since it’s your first time dropping it off late we’ll refund you “. I didn’t care they said that at least I got my money back right ? Wrong I was only refunded $53.62 .

title: Horrible Experience
text: Been a subscriber with Zipcar for over a year. Never had one good experience: - dirty cars - cars parking lots have no Internet coverage (good luck connect to the app to end or start a trip) - continuous technical issues with the app; when using the same account on different phones - bad customer service (most of the time)

title: Second rail in a week
text: I ordered a zipcar and while driving an alert for tire pressure popped up.  They told me to put air in tires.  It wad 20 degrees But I did.  Alert stayed on.  I advised I was concerned to drive.  Customer service was terrible.  Eventually roadside assistance showed up and told me someone had disabled the sensors:  so the alert was to be expected by zipcar.  He checked the tire pressure.  Or was 2x the appropriate pressure.  Everyone had a low tire pressure alert and was adding air.    Dangerous and horrible experience

title: 0/10 CUSTOMER SERVICE
text: Customer Service is extremely horrible they constantly shut off your cars in the middle of use which afterwards you have to call for them to restart the car  app is horrible everything about this organization is horrible all representatives have no clue what their doing or talking about just plain horrible

title: Price Gauging at its finest
text: Don’t bother with this service if you are looking for affordability because it’s almost certain that you will incur a fee for something within your first few trips $50 fee plus a 1/2 hour additional charge for being 1 minute late then $30 charge for gas that was charged 3 days after trip ended and gas is included in rental but the gauge read 30% fuel and even the app recorded a fuel level of 31-34%but 3 days later it decides it was actually below 25% it’s ridiculous there’s no warning for new account and no grace periods then they lie about it’s for customer disruptions that fees are assessed when it’s really about lining their pockets I received only a $7 credit for one of my trips because it wasn’t returned on time from previous driver but the driver was charged $64and the difference goes to zipcar it’s not worth it in the beginning of a new membership.

title: Horrible

They must be reporting bad reviews.

text: They switch my car location from right next to my house to a 25 minute drive away and won’t reimburse me for time or taxi to go pick it up, their cars often don’t work.  This company is absolute trash

title: Horrible Experience
text: My account was closed for no reason and no clarification provided. It was less than 24hrs of account creation, they charged full membership fees and monthly protection fees.

title: The app is not working correctly.
text: It's too expensive. Customer support isn't very good. If a user has problems with the car or the app, you'll have to wait a long time for help from customer support. The last time I called the support call center, I waited for 40 minutes. I couldn’t start the car because the app was glitching. Meanwhile, the rental time had already started, and I could only wait for a response from support. After 40 minutes, they answered the call and gave me another car from the same parking lot, adjusting the rental time to account for the delay. However, they didn’t refund me for the 40 minutes I had already spent waiting for support, and I was still charged for that time. Essentially, they just moved the rental period later, as if I had started the rental later, but I still paid for those 40 minutes of waiting. Later, when I tried to return the car on time, the app glitched again and wouldn’t let me end the rental, even though I arrived at the return location on time. The app kept tracking time and charged me for the extra minutes while I was trying to contact support again. Despite returning the car on time, they never refunded me for the extra charges for the time I couldn’t end the rental due to the app issues. It seems like this company is irresponsible toward its customers. They should follow basic service standards, like those in European car-sharing services. Despite the highest rental prices, this service is the most frustrating. Every time I used it, something went wrong.

title: App update makes Zipcar unusable
text: I’ve used Zipcar since before iPhones or apps. The latest version requires 2factor authentication; it texts a code to your phone. You have 30 seconds to enter the code. It takes 20 seconds to send the code and half the time there’s no keyboard and the other half of the time it doesn’t appear in the app meaning you need to retrieve it from texts. Good service just currently unusable.

title: Car in wrong place
text: The Zip Car service is fine but there was a car in my space and I had to park somewhere else

title: Spotty Customer Service - but a decent option if you need it.
text: The customer service is inconsistent. Some times I will get someone good but other times there will be people who don’t know how to solve your issues. However the product itself isn’t too bad.  I finished my ride but it turns out someone parked in my spot. I called the rep but he only told me to find a 24 hour garage and pay for it. Mind you I would have to uber back as well which they said they would not cover. The rep offered no other good option… I’m not sure the reps are well trained about these situations. Otherwise I liked the car itself and the ride. Pricing is high but it’s not too crazy.

title: Overseas customer service only Philippines
text: I was unable to extend the zipcar via the app, then i call spoke to a customer service overseas and told me that I was fine we just charge you for a half an hour then they charge me a $63  late fee, plus the the extra +30 fee🤦🏼‍♂️

title: Very convenient!!

title: Soo expensive

title: ABSOLUTE NIGHTMARE - STAY AWAY FROM THIS APP!
text: Where do I even begin with this disaster? First, their registration system is completely broken and poorly designed. Made ONE simple mistake with my phone number during registration? LOCKED OUT FOREVER. Their brilliant solution? Send pictures of myself holding my driver's license like I'm some criminal, plus utility bills and other personal documents through email - in 2025! Are we living in the stone age? The customer service is a whole other level of incompetence. They kept sending copy-paste responses for TWO WEEKS, completely ignoring the actual problem. And here's the kicker - when I finally gave up and asked to delete my account, they had the audacity to tell me it would take SIX MONTHS! Yes, you read that right. SIX. MONTHS. The UX is so horrifically bad that I couldn't even get past the login screen. Their form doesn't properly handle country codes, leading to verification failures, and there's absolutely no way to fix it without jumping through ridiculous hoops. Save yourself the headache and use literally ANY other car-sharing service. I've never seen such a backwards, user-hostile system in my life. Deleting this app and never looking back. Absolutely pathetic service in 2025."

title: Nice Ride
text: Always a nice smooth experience using zip car

title: Scam Alert: Stay Away from This App!
text: This app is nothing but a scam. Zipcar tricks you into paying a non-refundable application fee by allowing you to register with a non-UK number, only to reject your application later and demand a UK number. Even after following their instructions and fixing the issue, my account was still not approved, and they refused to refund my money.  Their customer service is completely useless—they hide behind policies instead of addressing their own mistakes. Zipcar takes your money and gives you nothing in return. Save yourself the frustration and avoid this app at all costs. There are far better car-sharing services out there.

title: Poor Review

title: Only use as a last resort; even then check ALL other options first.
text: The below is from a recent trip end report which is just one among many annoyances using this service. I’ve been using this service on and off for almost two years and it’s always been a pain.  Often cars are dirty and smelly also. And more than once l've had to refill nearly flat tires. In between personal cars I have to use it for longer errands but as soon as I have another option best believe this will be cancelled. Also you have to pay the $25 application fee anytime you cancel and reactivate and no options for pausing. Total ripoff and scam!!  When checking out the car, I had to do the full inspection 4-5 times because the app would not let me submit. Finally I started going through and choosing the boxes that would let me submit. Only certain combinations of checked box and text in box would let me submit. In the end in fact I don’t think I was even able to leave a text comment. Overall, if I didn’t need this service (and I will unsubscribe as soon as I don’t) I wouldn’t be using it at all. The experience itself plus customer service has been awful. I recently had to carry $1000 of damages on a work trip because the support team would not answer my questions or help out at all even though it was a hit and run at the location the zipcar was always parked. Absolute nightmare. If anything I’ll actively tell people NOT to use zipcar unless their life depended on it.

title: Terrible support
text: So many times your car doesn’t star, it’s out of gas, has a maintenance issue, been repeatedly hotboxed and trashed, leaves you abandoned somewhere, someone books right after you when you realize you need five more minutes, the gas card is missing or doesnt work and you have to go through a slow reimbursement process.   You have to fill up on gas on your own time. You have to fill up tires on your own time. End up being late for any of the issues above you get fined 50$ even though it’s a subscription service and youre paying the insurance and the cars are all booked when you need them.   Customer service can help solve your problems when you get to them but they never give you credits or refunds.   The system itself works and has helped me a lot but ive had several really bad experiences that i received no compensation or consideration for that has prompted me to leave this review.

title: App won’t let you cancel trip so Zipcar can charge you for it
text: App refuses to show upcoming trips you wish to cancel. The Zipcar charges you for them anyway.  Slick way to make money without wear and tear on the vehicles

title: No gas card ???
text: No gas card in vehicle

title: Uber that you walk to
text: I got hit with an $80+ fee for going over my time, when the sliders on the app were unresponsive and thus I was unable to change from the default one hour.  Honestly if you already have uber, just use that.  This can save you a few bucks if you’re going somewhere for a little while and don’t want to pay for an uber both ways, but uber won’t nail you with fees, for any reason that I’ve found.  Also, I couldn’t take pictures of the interior for the pre trip inspection because I took too long (more than 2 minutes) taking pictures for the exterior inspection, so that my doors locked with no way to re-open them except to skip through the rest of the inspection.  Lazy programming + punitive fees= pass on this app

title: Just bad

title: IT'S EVEN WORSE THAN 1/5

title: No customer service
text: I booked a car weeks ago. Went to the car the day do the first reservation and the car unlocked using the app. then the app glitches and needed me to sign in again and also needed a verification number sent to my phone which was not being sent. I spent 30 mins on a “10 minute wait priority for members” and when the representative gets on the call she simply says to continue with the reservation. I can not use the application to open and lock the car since the verification code was not being sent. She then put me on hold and did not return to the call. I had to call again and wait another 30mins for a representative to come on and I wasted an hour of the reservation time trying to fix this issue which made me late to my event. Once I am done with this reservation I am canceling and NEVER using it again. Don’t do it to yourself.

title: There’s no way Zipcar actually has 4.5 stars…
text: Picked up my rental for Thanksgiving and it was disgusting. Cigarette burn holes in the seats, stains all over the place, cigarette smell. The condition of the car is an issue too, the back left tire was so low on air I worried it was going to go flat during our hour drive to the family Thanksgiving. The bumper also looked like it was going to fall off. When I called Zipcar to complain and get a new car, they said all reservations were filled (which I figured given the holiday) but they would only give me an hour of credit to my account.  When I told the phone operator this was unacceptable and they needed to make it right, they hung up the phone.  Don’t waste your money here!!! Terrible rentals and terrible customer service.

title: Lack of car options

title: “Unreliable Service and Poor Customer Support”
text: I had a terrible experience with Zipcar on September 7th. I reserved a car that turned out not to exist, and the location was 45 minutes away from my home. I spent almost $65 traveling back and forth, only to find out two hours later that the previous renter hadn’t returned the car. Despite their promise of reimbursement, Zipcar has not compensated me and now claims there’s nothing they can do. This company shows a blatant disregard for their customers’ time and money. I strongly advise against using Zipcar for your rental needs. Their service is unreliable, and their customer service is non-existent.”

title: Worst customer service ever!!
text: I had a terrible experience with Zipcar. The app was completely unresponsive—I couldn’t extend my trip, submit a report, or contact support. The fuel card was missing in the car, and despite clicking multiple times, nothing worked. Now they’re charging me a late fee for something entirely caused by their faulty app. This was beyond frustrating, and the lack of reliable customer service made it even worse. Zipcar needs to fix their app and take responsibility for their tech issues instead of punishing customers. Very disappointing.

title: Just get Turo or a use rental place
text: The price for a zipcar for one day came out to over $500 just use a car rental place or app like Turo. The money for that actually goes to the people renting their cars out. Or a car rental place is more cost effective. This may be convenient but terrible car selection and unaccounted for prices pop up at the end.

title: Scam company, avoid at all cost!

title: Bad customer service
text: Expensive membership cost and hourly price, yet extremely bad customer support

title: Do not recommend
text: Horrible company and customer service

title: Crapshoot Capitalism
text: If you book, know that your car may not be there. When you get there, only to find it’s not, the response of this corporation is to refund what you paid and give you a credit for an additional hour, to be used within the next 60 days. But that one-hour credit doesn’t always reflect the loss one suffers financially from not having access to the transportation they paid for.    Why allow a customer to book a car the company knows is not available?   Having used the service a lot, it’s clear this is a business practice. The loss you suffer as a result is not one they’re willing to compensate quickly or easily.   Get a Lyft or a cab if you need reliability.

title: DO NOT USE FOR UBER!!!!!!
text: They will charge you $154 for being EXACTLY ontime and even 1 minute late.. they say call and tell them if you’re running behind so they can not charge you but THEY STILL CHARGE YOU! Here is my situation, I booked the car for a couple of days to do Uber. As time is getting closer to drop back off the car I attempted to extend it, it lets me. (This is where it gets very very frustrating and honestly a scam). I then get a notification at 2:14pm while doing a Uber ride (mind you the car WAS due at 2:30pm). So I call and get everything clarified, was told that I won’t be charged and if so call them and get it fixed. So when I dropped the car off and got into another I waited 2 hours just to see who booked my car when there was 2 other cars available. Waited, and waited, finally I check the app to see if the car was really booked… lol no one booked the car, I booked it again and they cancelled my reservation the same day. Asked why and get told a different answer everytime. Now I call to ask for the $154 to be removed and guess what I’m told? Sorry we can’t do that. I pointed out the notes that were left and still get told no we can’t do that. Then I mentioned what occurred and that the car is still there unbooked even tho it showed I was booked in it, then someone else overrided my bookin, SOMEHOW-still don’t know how that was possible. NOW, the car is not booked and free?!?! You see the scam in all of this, almost like they wanted not only the $99 I paid to rebook but charged me an extra $154 to get even more money from me. If it’s not removed by 9pm Oct. 29 I am taking Zipcar and whatever corporation that runs them to court. I’m sure I’m not the first this has happened to but I will promise you, I will be the last.  Update: 3 agents hung up on me in my face after saying they’ll speak to a manger. I am respectful, I did not raise my voice, I am just looking for a solution that I know can be done.. but I tried playing it nice, you guys still owe ME money so I’m just going to test my luck in court. -do not partner with this company. I as well as others will be making complaints to Uber as well as fulfilling a lawsuit.

title: Worst service ever!!!
text: Beware of high extra hidden charges and fees. Poor maintenance putting lives at risk.

title: Terrible service
text: The app worked but the zip are service is terrible. There were issues with the car we rented, then the customer service was terrible. We lost half the days running around New York trying to get a car, they wanted to charge us for the 3 hrs we spent trying to sort out their issues. Just rent a car at a regular car rental place. Zipcar isn’t worth it.

title: Perfect transportation option
text: Clean, easy to rent, nice vehicle choices

title: Give me a refund for the unproven service.
text: Hello, how do I get my money back? I was in New York for 1 day, I wanted to rent a car for a day. But for some reason, you first write off the money and then check the documents. title: They waste your time and then call you late.
text: Even if you return the car before its return time, the app then asks you to take several photos, which is difficult and time consuming in the tight parking spaces you’ll find these cars in; once you’re done, the app will say “you were only a few minutes late” and then say they’ll close your account if they see a pattern for rule breaking. Most likely the account closure for “lateness” would be automatic for a tech oriented company like this, so your only option is to return the car early, with an open ended buffer based on whatever return process is implemented in the app, which could fluctuate unexpectedly in the amount of time it takes. The return time should be based on when I tap the “end trip” button, with the lengthy return process not counting toward that, unless I stop interacting with the app and it times out or something, which should then require me to tap the “end trip” button again. It’s common sense from a UX perspective.

title: Unreliable cars and jaded customer support
text: I’ve been a Zipcar member for over 15 years, and lately there has been a massive uptick in the amount of members failing to return cars on time.  I’m not talking about 5-10min late, I’m talking about HOURS.   In the 15 years I’ve been a member, I had never once experienced this once, and then suddenly in the last few months it’s happened 3-4x with the SAME location.  Zipcar used to drop members who did this, but clearly that’s not happening.  They need to increase the late fee to $500 and drop members who do it more than 2x in a 12mo period–that would probably eliminate the problem altogether.    Worse, when you call Zipcar customer support, they are super blasé about it.   I feel like that $50 late fee should be given to the member it impacted.  But customer support doesn’t seem to care.  They offer to try to find you another car nearby, often one of lesser quality and multiple blocks away—which is a problem for me as a disabled person.  Then, they proceed to charge you the SAME fee as if you got your original rental—not discounting it at all.  Even better, you actually have to ASK them to change the reservation to remove the period you paid for that the car wasn’t available for.  Today was the final straw and I cancelled my membership.  Zipcar used to be great, but it’s terrible now.

title: Horrible
text: I am incredibly disappointed with Zipcar's customer service and policies. Recently, I was charged a $50 late fee despite being in the parking lot with the vehicle, simply because I hadn't clicked the "return car" button on the app. The reason for this delay? I was thoroughly cleaning the car, far better than how I received it, in an attempt to set a higher standard of cleanliness for other users.  As someone who always strives to be a good customer and do the right thing, this treatment feels like a punishment. I was in the parking lot, with no customers waiting, ensuring the car was spotless. Yet, Zipcar showed no understanding or flexibility regarding my situation.  Charging a $50 late fee under these circumstances is absolutely ridiculous and shows a lack of appreciation for customers who go the extra mile. Zipcar, this is not how you retain loyal customers. I expected better understanding and support, but instead, I am left feeling frustrated and undervalued.

title: Nightmarish Zipcar Experience Portland
text: Buyer beware!  I have been using zip car since 2021 in Portland, OR.My rental experience has progressively gotten worse over time until now, in May of 2024, when I am seriously considering unsubscribing from them. I tend to rent a car from them at least twice a month.  The following factors are present in about 75% of the cars I rent from them:  1. Cleanliness: Pet hair on seats, floor, or trunk Cigarette burns on seats Stains on seats Sand on seats Sand in trunk Trash on seats Stains on center console  Trash left in trunk Trash left under the seats Trash left in door storage areas  Typically several of these cleanliness factors are at play. I rarely encounter a zip car that is relatively clean.  There is zero accountability for the filth and dirtiness that these customers tend to leave behind.  2. Issues with app: App routinely fails to properly communicate with car locks so you are unable to end the trip because the app will not lock the doors. App must be restarted because bluetooth lock command fails to communicate with the car  3. Issues with customer support Routinely deal with 20-40 minute wait times when issues with the car occur. Time of day doesn’t matter nor does priority customer route make a difference. If you have an issue with your current car and need another one, customer support reps tend not to compensate you for lost time with more time on your active trip; rather, they give you a credit for the next trip.  If you shorten your trip by accident and then end it, they will not refund you some of the trip.    4. Issues with parking spots Zip car parking spots are poorly marked with small signage. People often trespass by parking their personal vehicles in your reserved zip car spots, forcing you to park elsewhere and notify zip car of your new parking location. You are not compensated for this time wasted.  5. Issues with the cars: Cars will have issues with tires, external damage, warning lights on dashboard, etc (caused by previous customers) which I have reported in the past repeatedly with the same cars. Zipcar does not repair them nor take them out of service after my reports. They continued to rent them out despite having some of these issues. They appear to care more about the bottom line than the safety of their customers  In conclusion, the zipcars in Portland tend to be filthy and disgusting and will typically have some external or internal damage.  If that isn’t an issue, the app typically will malfunction by failing to communicate with the locks on the car.  Additionally, customer service will invariably try to cheat you out of your money by giving you a credit on future trips.  This service in it’s current state is nearly unusable.  I suggest you stay away from it.

title: Terrible app

text: I rented a car for about a week and it was too expensive. The rates change per day and Zip Car doesn't offer weekly rates. This may be a good option if someone wanted to drive for a few days. Everything is remote and no local office or help. You have to call to extend for a day or more instead of using the app. The car needed to be detailed and missing parts such as the cap for the charge plug. It is a newer model electric car. Zip Car pays for the charging with EVGo but you can't get any help from EVGo if you have an issue at the charging station. I tried and I couldn’t verify their account. Customer support is outsourced and they don't know much or help with anything. One day the car wouldnt start. I’m going to rent a car through Avis for less than what I paid ZipCar. Renting should only be temporary because the companies are the only ones that profit.

title: Overcharged Resolution
text: Made a rental for 24 hours. Booking issues occurred and the System sets it only for 1 hour and upon calling to check issues. Everything seemed to be fine but the ride was shut off and  charged me a late fee claiming the car not being returned in time;Towed the vehicle. Within a couple of days and help from upper management and the issue has been resolved. Nice addition option to have. Customer service available on phone and has a chat support. I’d advise to reach out timely upon issues and double check booking immediately after creating one. Thanks to support for Helping once again

title: MUST READ
text: Okay so let’s start with bad & end on a good note.  I do not like that there’s a younger age fee.. makes absolutely no sense. A weekend rental went from $195 to $723… absolutely insane! That’s not including insurance. Instead of charging a younge fee.. automatically put the option to add insurance!!! So in reality there actually losing money & potential customers because of this dumb idea.  Another I DO NOT LIKE!!! Is that when they mess up it’s no problem and we gotta deal with it. But if you mess up , your no longer able to rent cars from Zipcar. Why is this fair? Well it isn’t. Then have strong accent employees who get frustrated when there the ones who should speak clear because we are the customers seeking help.   When I use to use my friends account it was better, cheap prices for cars. Makes it affordable and makes me want to keep it longer. So now I’m investing more money, which is smart, because the company ;Zipcar) grows financially.   I do think they should rethink alotttt of things. Maybe revamp the site. My idea is truly the smartest’ lol. Because not only are you making money but your growing with customers!

title: Worst Customer Experience
text: For one I will say the booking is convenient yet the actual functionality of the app is horrible . Trying to get help with agents takes hours sometimes and then once connected you are treated horribly. I would NOT recommend this service AT ALL!!! They charge you $50 for being 1 minute late but have no help when they can’t find the car you booked . This is truly the worst car service ever and I will not be using again!

title: The worst customer support
text: They have the worst customer support and do not care about the customer at all.

title: Very bad service
text: The customer service is very bad. Very disappointed with zipcar service!!!

title: Disgusting company
text: I live in the city and gave up my car, figuring the few times I needed a car I could use zip car. That was my first mistake. Every single car I got smelled of disgusting cigarettes. Most of the cars were trashed with garbage everywhere, windows smeared and crumbs everywhere. Several of the cars had broken headlights, flat tires and smashed bumpers. Customer service takes forever to get ahold of and does little to help when you get in touch with them.   Bottom line, I subscribed to this service for less hassle, only it’s way more because of the condition of the cars.

title: Major loss of functionality
text: The app recently downgraded it’s functionality when it changed the interface for taking photos of the vehicle at the start and end of the rental period. Formerly, it was possible to select your camera’s wide angle lens to properly capture the car. Now it is not possible to do so. The prior version also provided you with the sections of the car’s exterior to document. No longer there. Most notably, the new version consistently fails to let you proceed past the exterior photos. Did anyone actually test this before release?

title: App not working
text: My app isn’t letting me Log In. I have an active account and paying for service. I cannot access at all from the app. I had tried deleting app, changing email, password and everything else. Nothing let me log in on the app. I can use browser but can’t open a car from the phone.  I hace iPhone 15 & last apple version.  Completely useless now my account. Thanks a lot. 😤

title: Unable to reopen account
text: Unfortunate experience, after being a loyal customer with Zipcar and relying on its services to transport my children to school & my sick grandma to and from appointments, there are times were I brought reservations a little bit past the drop off time. However, being a customer that has spent over $6,000 using the service, I would’ve hoped to receive better customer service when attempting to reopen my closed account. The team is not offering any type of way for me to reopen my account, they blankly tell me it’s permanent and I should consider other car sharing options. Highly disappointing, and I wish there was a way for members to have their account closure reconsidered in a more generous way. I wish Zipcar could give my family another chance at having reliable transportation.

title: UNRELIABLE !!!!!
text: ZIP CAR IS THE ABSOLUTE WORSE I BOOKED MULTIPLE VEHICLES WITH ZIP CAR AND HAD NO ISSUE THEN THEY STARTED RENTING VEHICLES THAT WERE NEVER THERE THE FIRST TIME I WAS UNDERSTANDING THEN IT KEPT HAPPENING THEY KEPT RENTING VEHICLES AND SAYING THAT IT WAS IN ONE LOCATION AND WAS NEVER THERE THEN THEY WOULD SAY IM SO SORRY FOR THE INCONVENIENCE BUT THE VEHICLE IS ACTUALLY NO WHERE NEAR YOU THAT IS COMPLETELY UNPROFESSIONAL AND UNACCEPTABLE I SWEAR TO GOD DO NOT BOOK WITH THEM AND I WILL BE BRINGING THIS TO ZIP CAR CORPORATE THIS COMPANY IS THE WORST!!!

title: Nickel and diming and no customer service
text: I re-initiated my account and I had to pay $30 to sign up again. I feel that is egregious. Then I booked a car and needed to push back the start time. It charged me $7 to adjust the time.   I called customer service and they said if I would’ve called instead of using the app they wouldn’t of charged me to change the pick up time. That makes no sense. They shouldn’t charge you either way.   Then the person hung up on me.   What a waste

title: Customer service gives a hope
text: Was getting errors at the checkout, tried multiple cards, ended up rescheduling my trip. But support is decent

title: Zipcar service
text: Customer support is horrible. Satellite connection is poor Only thing zipcar cares about is your money!

title: Stuck in the middle of the street
text: So I rented a Kia Soul and everything was going good until late in the evening when I stopped at a red light and when I tried to drive it wouldn’t move.  So here I am stuck in the middle of the road and blocking traffic, so frustrating! I put it in park and then back into drive and the darn thing started rolling backwards smh I had to use the emergency break cause the break petal wouldn’t budge, thank goodness I didn’t hit the person behind me. Luckily a nice guy was driving by helped push me into the gas station lot that was right there. This experience has made me nervous about renting another vehicle from this app.

title: Finicky Bluetooth unlock
text: I’ve been stranded twice now because the app doesn’t work. Bring back the unlock cards!!

title: great concept - terrible service
text: Had panned a trip with my family to go for a birthday party. Have a new born so booked a SUV for safety reasons. Zipcar cancelled the trip the day before. The reinstated it. Then changed the car. The changed the location of the pick up. And all only over email that i had to find in my spam. After spending an hour to get a SUV, they moved the location. James Donovan from supprt said they would cover the Uber to pick up the car and get home given the change in location which i did. After claiming the Uber trip because of Zipcar changes - they denied the refund even after James has clearly said in an email that it would be refunded. Ridiculous and terrible experience. Never recommend this to anyone who wants some peace of mind and a fair treatment.

title: Non-refundability of membership/activation is a scam
text: As many other customers I saw their prices as quite reasonable before joining zipcar only get 30+% higher prices at the checkout and thus having wasted the 35$ for the membership fee and activation…

title: Buggin’
text: Guys, your app got more Bugs than Looney Tunes…

title: Customer Service intentionally doesn’t help after 40 minutes of keeping me on the line
text: It seems odd but the customer service attitude towards the customer is plain dismissive and never supporting.  I got a late fee of $50 when my phone died and took me hours to recharge and although I had dropped the car I couldn’t end the trip. After spending 40 minutes with a customer service agent who was keep vanishing on me I was told nothing can be done. This happened on two other occasions just this week. I think the app basically doesn’t allow the support agents to do anything that involves refunds. And the app does have issues, fails to extend, etc. And the $12, $16 and $50 fees add up…

title: First Ride App Doesn’t Work
text: I downloaded the app, signed up and reserved my first car. I go to pick up the car and I can’t sign onto the app. I tried resetting my password three times, uninstalling and reinstalling the app and neither would work. I called customer service and they weren’t able to help me. They refunded my trip, which is great and all, but I still needed a car for the day.  I cancelled my account without ever using it because it’s not reliable.

title: I was unknowingly charged 50$ multiple
text: They charged my account multiple times with 50 dollar late fee for being 5 minutes late for my return location. I am very regular customer, When I chatted with customer support, the agent was very unsupportive. I’ve cancelled my zip car membership. Terrible experience

title: Cars often need repairs, maintenance, and smell like smoke
text: We have tried Zipcar a three times now and have yet to have a good experience.   The first time our car was in a gated lot and we were not able to get out of the lot. After an hour of wasted time and talking to customer support they realized the building’s management had changed the gating system and they forgot to update their zipcars.  The next time we took a Zipcar a bit farther out to go for a hike and realized the car had no key, and we could only get into the car using our cell phone. We were concerned at the trailhead we might not have service and called support, and they confirmed we couldn’t get in without cell service and suggested we park somewhere else… what a useless suggestion. I’m not comfortable taking a car that doesn’t come with a key, if your phone dies or you don’t have service you are screwed.  Most recently we took a car again and upon picking it up it reeked of cigarette smoke. We didn’t want to cancel our plans so reported it in the pre trip inspection and went anyways. 5 minutes into the trip we got a warning that the rear tire was at 15 PSI, so we had to go well out of our way (not trivial in NYC) to get air for the tire. Zipcar wouldn’t refund us for any time because we didn’t call them during the issue and request a new car. Given that’s their policy I think we are done with Zipcar, it’s not reliable enough to really plan for anything because if there is an issue they want you to call them and wait around for them to try and find you some other car you need to walk blocks over to. We just paid 150 bucks to take a stinky car for 6 hours and spend 30 minutes of that time finding a gas station in NYC to pump up the tire, or cancel our plans for the day. We could just Uber for less money and fewer problems…

title: Terrible experience
text: Terrible referral system and customer support.

title: Stay Away!
text: They have a glitchy app that doesn’t accurately log drop off location or reservation times. Have been charged nearly a $100 late fee for a late that I didn’t commit. Terrible experience and customer service won’t help.

text: These cars have technology issues and when you reach out about them, they do not give you a refund. I had trouble connecting with a car to the point that I had to take an Uber. I Will be reporting them to the better business burea

title: TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE
text: I loved the actual service and had been a member for years!!! Like when they first started. title: Horrible customer service and user interface
text: I live in a college town. I understand there’s many young drivers so cars will be damaged and have odors. I can excuse that. What I can’t excuse is the abysmal functioning of this application. I am currently locked out awaiting customer service in the freezing cold because my application will not display my reservation at all, despite it showing up on the website, and I can’t get into the car. When I deleted and redownloaded the app, it straight up locked me out of my account and won’t allow me to log in. I’ve had times in the past where the map malfunctions and sends me to the wrong return spot, resulting in a late fee. This is atrocious and feels like preying on the young college kids without cars that depend on the service to get around.

title: Warning
text: If you cancel your reservation there is a message like “you will not receive a full refund” what it should read is “you will not receive any refund”. There’s a big difference. Frustrating after being a user for 10 years.

title: 50% reliable

I end up needing to call for over half of the trips I take with Zipcar, and they’re always “experiencing elevated call volume.”  This company doesn’t care about self service or great customer support, and I guess it must not be good economics.  Anyways, give yourself an extra hour or two to deal with Zipcar.  Definitely 2 stars.
